Softball pitcher Courtney Kessell, Kailua High School's freshman sensation year ago, would have nothing sophomore jinx.
Kessell evoked high after impressive state tournament last season which nearly Surfriders This year, Kessell only those have even surpassed them helping Kailua (16-0) first state title since 1997.
strong finish 2002 elevated from honorable mention status O'ahu Eastern Division spot all-state first team, recently completed season lofted into State Player Year after vote coaches media.
"She press, lived it," Kamehameha coach Sing Chow said after Kessell pitched struck eight state victory over Warriors.
Kessell joined all-state first team teammates Loke Lastimosa, Danielle Young Kamaile Hughes. Lastimosa, senior, selected third baseman although finished season shortstop.
fielded both positions well.
Young, senior, patrolled center field with similar range strong arm, provided solid hitting good speed from leadoff spot Surfriders' potent batting lineup.
Hughes, sophomore, batted cleanup threat clear fence time came bat. .435 during season.
Kamehameha Mililani each placed three players first team. Warriors were sophomore second baseman Liane Horiuchi, junior catcher Kaulana Gould junior utility player Brandi Peiler.
Horiuchi took over 2002 All-State second baseman Samantha Sardinha also replaced current University Hawai'i infielder Ashley Ruff leadoff batter. filled both roles admirably, hitting .395 during League Honolulu season with four home runs, runs RBIs stealing bases well providing solid defense.
Gould batted .361 with eight RBIs showed strong throwing base stealers.
Peiler first-team all-state outfielder previous years this season came through with several clutch pitching including shutout victory over Punahou game. perhaps state's most complete player, offering strong defensive play left field batting .462 during regular season, also going pitcher with perfect game no-hitter, plus combined no-hitter. also pitched shutout Mililani state semifinals.
Mililani's all-state trio consists junior shortstop Charity Senas, senior center fielder B.J. Shimamoto senior pitcher Miki Asamura. Senas showed great range strong field offered power hitting speed leadoff batter. batted .565 during season with five home runs RBIs.
Shimamoto .300 with eight stolen bases pressure pitchers defenses with speed. three-time Advertiser All-State selection.
Asamura, another repeat selection, went during season with 0.42 strikeouts. Shimamoto Asamura will play University
Kaimuki first baseman Tyleen Tausaga Baldwin center fielder Zoie Sevilla, both repeat selections, round first team. Tausaga again OIA's most feared sluggers signed with Sevilla, speedy defensive player with strong quick bat, headed UH-Hilo.
Kailua's Bernard Victor named Coach Year after guiding Surfriders unscathed through tough East schedule, playoffs state tournament.
